topic.what: Comprehensive review of Rikkunshito (Liu Jun Zi Tang) as a Kampo herbal formula for functional dyspepsia, synthesizing available clinical trial data and mechanistic evidence.
topic.how: Systematic narrative review of clinical trials and preclinical studies examining ghrelin signaling enhancement, gastric accommodation improvement, and anti-inflammatory effects of Rikkunshito.
topic.why_matters: Functional dyspepsia affects up to 20% of the global population with limited pharmacological options; understanding Rikkunshito's multi-target mechanisms provides a framework for integrating traditional East Asian medicine into evidence-based gastroenterology practice.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability of Rikkunshito to upregulate acyl-ghrelin levels and potentiate GHS-R1a receptor signaling
characteristics.value: Consistent ghrelin elevation across multiple preclinical and clinical studies
characteristics.evidence: Multiple RCTs and animal studies cited in review; doi:10.3389/fphar.2021.640576
characteristics.context: Ghrelin enhancement is the primary proposed mechanism for prokinetic and appetite-stimulating effects

characteristics.what_it_measures: Enhancement of fundic relaxation in response to meal ingestion, reducing postprandial discomfort
characteristics.value: Significant improvement reported in clinical studies
characteristics.context: Relevant for postprandial distress syndrome subtype of functional dyspepsia

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction of mucosal inflammation markers in gastric tissue
characteristics.context: May contribute to symptom relief in Helicobacter pylori-associated dyspepsia

good_for: Patients with functional dyspepsia, especially postprandial distress syndrome
Patients with low ghrelin levels or impaired gastric accommodation
Researchers seeking mechanistic overview of Rikkunshito
not_for: Patients outside the clinical trial population demographics
Direct mechanistic discovery (review only, not original research)
requires: Access to Kampo herbal preparations meeting Japanese pharmacopoeia standards
Clinical context distinguishing functional from organic dyspepsia
Understanding of ghrelin pathway biology for mechanistic interpretation

novelty: Synthesizes ghrelin-centric mechanism with gastric motility and anti-inflammatory axes into unified framework
Provides first comprehensive English-language review of Rikkunshito RCT evidence for functional dyspepsia
Positions Liu Jun Zi Tang / Rikkunshito as a cross-cultural formula amenable to modern pharmacological explanation
open_problems: Optimal dosing and treatment duration remain undefined in Western populations
Head-to-head comparison with standard prokinetics (e.g., domperidone, itopride) lacking
Long-term safety data and interactions with conventional medications not fully characterized
Mechanism of gastric accommodation improvement at molecular level incompletely elucidated
